one_line_reason: The first target that's the right shape — a web app your team can own and scale, so the competence gate finally PASSES — but almost certainly abandoned pre-revenue code: zero public footprint, no metrics disclosed, red-ocean category. Only worth it if the seller proves a real, engaged userbase.

build_scale_fit.note: A productivity web app is dead-center in the team's wheelhouse — they can own, modernize, add AI, and scale it. Competence gate PASSES (first of the three candidates).

pmf_retention.note: No users/revenue disclosed and zero public footprint; Pomodoro/focus tools are notoriously low-retention. No evidence of PMF.

unit_economics.note: $1.5K price + no metrics = almost certainly pre-revenue; monetizing focus timers is hard (low willingness to pay).

moat_channel.note: Red-ocean category (Pomofocus, Forest, Session, Flow, Todoist, Notion); no moat, and no visible acquisition channel.

growth_market.note: Huge but saturated market; an AI 'focus coach' angle is possible but everyone is doing it.

tech_ip.note: It's a web app the team could own — but the site returned an empty shell and the codebase/stack/ownership is unverified (could be a template).

deal_quality.note: $1,500 is cheap in absolute terms, but poor value for a team that can build a Pomodoro app in a weekend — unless real users come with it.

seller_risk.note: 'Offloading distracting projects to focus on e-commerce' = abandoned hobby; withholding all metrics strongly implies ~no revenue.

key_unknowns: Are there ANY real, engaged users / any revenue? This is the whole deal — with none, you're buying rebuildable code. Swings from Pass to Promising.
Retention: do users come back after a few weeks? Pomodoro tools churn hard; decisive for whether there's a business.
Is there a functioning app at all (fetch returned an empty shell) and is 100% of the code owned + transferable (not a paid template/boilerplate)?
Any acquisition channel with traction? Zero public footprint suggests none.
Ongoing costs/liabilities (hosting, APIs) and whether any paying user transfers.
seller_questions: The decisive one: current registered users, MAU/WAU, and any revenue/MRR — screen-shared from analytics + Stripe/billing. (PMF + unit economics + revenue verification)
Retention: of users who sign up, what share are still active at 4 weeks? (retention)
Where do users come from and how many visits/month — organic, Product Hunt, ads, none? (channel; there's no public footprint, so this reveals whether any distribution exists)

Exactly what transfers: repo/codebase, domain, and the user database — and is 100% of the code owned by you (no license-limited template/boilerplate)? What's the stack/hosting? (tech + IP transferability)
How long has it been live, is there any monetization today, and why sell at $1,500? (adverse selection)
Monthly running costs and any third-party dependencies (auth, payments, APIs)? (economics)
inputs_provided: Listing: 'web-app for organizing your workload and prompting you to work in a productive digital environment' [seller-stated]
Asking price $1,500, sale via Reddit DM [seller-stated]
Reason for sale: offloading distracting projects to focus on e-commerce [seller-stated -> abandonment signal]
No revenue, traffic, or user numbers disclosed [OBSERVED — notable omission]
pomocalm.com returned an empty shell on fetch; not verified as a functioning app [OBSERVED]
Public search for 'pomocalm': zero footprint — no Product Hunt, reviews, or mentions [OBSERVED -> no traction signal]
